merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established between the United States and Moldova, prior to the opening of the embassy.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established when President George H.W. Bush and Moldovan President Mircea Snegur agreed to do so during a February 18, 1992 meeting at the White House. The Bush administration confirmed February 18 as the date relations were established in a statement issued the next day.

merged_events.event_description: The American Embassy in Chisinau was established, with Howard Steers as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: The American Embassy in Chisinau was established on March 13, 1992, with Howard Steers as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. diplomatic presence established in Moldova with a Chargé d'Affaires ad interim presenting credentials.

evidence.quote: Howard Steers, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1992-03-13.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Embassy established in Moldova with the first Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary presenting credentials.

evidence.quote: Mary C Pendleton, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1992-08-11. Credentials presented: 1992-08-26. Left post on 1995-08-12.

matched.notes: CSV matches RDCR Event 1 (Embassy established 1992-03-13) exactly. POCOM Event 2 (same date, labeled Liaison) and Event 3 (1992-08-26, Ambassador credentials) both refer to the same mission but reflect personnel milestones rather than separate institutional transitions. RDCR is primary and explicitly states the Embassy was established on 1992-03-13.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Diplomatic relations were established between the United States and Moldova during a meeting between President Bush and Moldovan President Snegur at the White House on February 18, 1992.
missing_from_csv.notes: RDCR explicitly states this as the date diplomatic relations were established, prior to the embassy opening on March 13. This could be added as a 'None' status event representing the formal establishment of relations before any diplomatic mission was set up.
